title: Understand area
description: Practice finding the area of different figures.

assessment_items.question_content:
**Which shapes have an area of $3$ square units?**

[[☃ image 1]]

is $1$ square unit.

[[☃ radio 1]]
hints:
**Area** is the space a flat shape fills.

To measure area, we can count the number of **same-sized** square units needed to cover a shape. The squares should have **no gaps or overlaps**.

[[☃ image 1]]

is $1$ **square unit** because it measures $1$ unit on each side.

This rectangle has $3$ square units covering it, but there are overlaps:

[[☃ image 1]]

This rectangle has $3$ square units covering it. There are no gaps or overlaps.

[[☃ image 1]]

So, this rectangle has an area of $3$ square units.

This rectangle has $3$ square units covering it, but there are overlaps:

[[☃ image 1]]

This rectangle has $3$ square units covering it. There are no gaps or overlaps.


[[☃ image 1]]

So, this rectangle has an area of $3$ square units.

These rectangles have an area of $3$ square units:


[[☃ image 1]]

[[☃ image 2]]

assessment_items.question_content:
**Which shapes have an area of $8$ square units?**

![A square.](web+graphie:${☣ LOCALPATH}/images/af46131ca093f4eec39e439a5be61421fe4f1048) is $1$ square unit.

[[☃ radio 1]]
hints:
**Area** is the space a flat shape fills.

To measure area, we can count the number of **same-sized** square units needed to cover a shape. The squares should have **no gaps or overlaps**.

![A square.](web+graphie:${☣ LOCALPATH}/images/af46131ca093f4eec39e439a5be61421fe4f1048) is $1$ **square unit** because it measures $1$ unit on each side.

This shape has $8$ square units covering it, but there are gaps:

![A rectangle made up of two rows with equal-sized squares. The top row has four squares and four missing rectangles. The bottom row has four squares and four missing rectangles.](web+graphie:${☣ LOCALPATH}/images/d8c21f291edc05e66f27bb64b4883a98ee157a8c)

This shape has $8$ square units covering it, but there are gaps and overlaps:

![A square made up of three rows of equal-sized squares. The top and bottom rows have two squares each with one missing square. in each row. The second row has no squares. There is a square made up of 2 rows with 2 squares in each row that is centered in and overlaps the three rows of the larger square.](web+graphie:${☣ LOCALPATH}/images/0c6cd3aa7934d8c6fdeec0a6f148077ba8f66042)

This shape has $8$ square units covering it. There are no gaps or overlaps.

![2 rows of equal-sized squares connected together. The first rows 2 squares. The second row has 6 squares.](web+graphie:${☣ LOCALPATH}/images/fe93d2df1869d5f771820c89baf25a8498b1bebe) 

So, this shape has an area of $8$ square units.

This shape has $8$ square units covering it. There are no gaps or overlaps.

![Four rows of equal-sized squares connected together. The top row has one square. The second and third row have three squares each. The fourth row has one square.](web+graphie:${☣ LOCALPATH}/images/d2d1a74125c9e7fbfb8f5a00c87aaaa75fe35a5a)

So, this shape has an area of $8$ square units.

These shapes have an area of $8$ square units:  
![2 rows of equal-sized squares connected together. The first rows 2 squares. The second row has 6 squares.](web+graphie:${☣ LOCALPATH}/images/fe93d2df1869d5f771820c89baf25a8498b1bebe) ![Four rows of equal-sized squares connected together. The top row has one square. The second and third row have three squares each. The fourth row has one square.](web+graphie:${☣ LOCALPATH}/images/d2d1a74125c9e7fbfb8f5a00c87aaaa75fe35a5a)

choices.content: ![Two rows of equal-sized squares connected together. The first row has two squares. The second row has six squares.](web+graphie:${☣ LOCALPATH}/images/fe93d2df1869d5f771820c89baf25a8498b1bebe)

![A rectangle made up of two rows with equal-sized squares. The top row has four squares and four missing rectangles. The bottom row has four squares and four missing rectangles.](web+graphie:${☣ LOCALPATH}/images/d8c21f291edc05e66f27bb64b4883a98ee157a8c)

assessment_items.question_content:
**Which shapes have an area of $12$ square units?**

![A square.](web+graphie:${☣ LOCALPATH}/images/913132d526938cf267deaa69928edc2351e20d30) is $1$ square unit.

[[☃ radio 1]]
hints:
**Area** is the space a flat shape fills.

To measure area, we can count the number of **same-sized** square units needed to cover a shape. The squares should have **no gaps or overlaps**.

![A square.](web+graphie:${☣ LOCALPATH}/images/913132d526938cf267deaa69928edc2351e20d30) is $1$ **square unit** because it measures $1$ unit on each side.

This shape has $12$ square units covering it, but there are gaps:

![A rectangle made up of three rows with equal-sized squares. Each row has four squares and four missing rectangles.](web+graphie:${☣ LOCALPATH}/images/dd921615637cbc3863b1c32159712e449baecff2)

This shape has $12$ square units covering it, but there are gaps and overlaps:

![This shape is a square with a smaller missing square in the lower left corner. Twelve squares that are the same size as the missing square are overlapping and partially cover the shape leaving several gaps.](web+graphie:${☣ LOCALPATH}/images/4b89661656954700efd23d1122d50c284848dbd4)

This shape has $12$ square units covering it. There are no gaps or overlaps.

![A rectangle made up of four rows of equal-sized squares. Each row has 3 squares.](web+graphie:${☣ LOCALPATH}/images/1492c1549f8175f29b9a5e640381591267c75844)

So, this shape has an area of $12$ square units.

This shape has $12$ square units covering it. There are no gaps or overlaps.

![A rectangle made up of two rows of equal-sized squares. Each row has six squares.](web+graphie:${☣ LOCALPATH}/images/95e5bd74ab5bd9fe72432d840281f557bd1ff8fa)

So, this shape has an area of $12$ square units.

These shapes have an area of $12$ square units:

![A rectangle made up of four rows of equal-sized squares. Each row has 3 squares.](web+graphie:${☣ LOCALPATH}/images/1492c1549f8175f29b9a5e640381591267c75844) ![A rectangle made up of two rows of equal-sized squares. Each row has six squares.](web+graphie:${☣ LOCALPATH}/images/95e5bd74ab5bd9fe72432d840281f557bd1ff8fa)

assessment_items.question_content:
**Which shapes have an area of $4$ square units?**

![A square.](web+graphie:${☣ LOCALPATH}/images/e84bf2b2e3d1e5e75c9583276f68463f72f10826) is $1$ square unit.

[[☃ radio 1]]
hints:
**Area** is the space a flat shape fills.

To measure area, we can count the number of **same-sized** square units needed to cover a shape. The squares should have **no gaps or overlaps**.

![A square.](web+graphie:${☣ LOCALPATH}/images/e84bf2b2e3d1e5e75c9583276f68463f72f10826) is $1$ **square unit** because it measures $1$ unit on each side.

This shape has $4$ square units covering it. There are no gaps or overlaps.

![A figure made up of three rows of equal-sized squares connected together. The first row has one square, the second row has one square, and the third row has two squares.](web+graphie:${☣ LOCALPATH}/images/e6030a1279acb9ae6e74e4ba6796f9f72f5bde1b)

So, this shape has an area of $4$ square units.

This shape has $4$ square units covering it. There are no gaps or overlaps.

![A figure made up of three rows of equal-sized squares connected together. The first row has one square, the second row has two squares, and the third row has one square.](web+graphie:${☣ LOCALPATH}/images/e9b17ef7efb1aaeff6dc497076d507db7220f0f0)

So, this shape has an area of $4$ square units.

This shape has $6$ square units covering it, not $4$:
  
![A figure made up of three rows of equal-sized squares connected together. The first row has two squares, the second row has two squares, and the third row has two squares.](web+graphie:${☣ LOCALPATH}/images/a630be0d498de18f3fd6a01af2a8c4d383992cfb)

This shape has $16$ square units covering it, not $4$:
  
![A figure made up of four rows of equal-sized squares connected together. The first row has four squares, the second row has four squares, the third row has four  squares, and the fourth row has four squares.](web+graphie:${☣ LOCALPATH}/images/9303324551bae0fc284468227c9124529e47c996)

These shapes have an area of $4$ square units:

![Two figures are shown.  The first figure is made up of three rows of equal-sized squares connected together. The first row has one square, the second row has two squares, and the third row has one square. The second figure is made up of three rows of equal-sized squares connected together. The first row has one square, the second row has one square, and the third row has two squares.](web+graphie:${☣ LOCALPATH}/images/e9b17ef7efb1aaeff6dc497076d507db7220f0f0)  ![](web+graphie:${☣ LOCALPATH}/images/e6030a1279acb9ae6e74e4ba6796f9f72f5bde1b)

hints:
**Area** is the space a flat shape fills.

To measure area, we can count the number of **same-sized** square units needed to cover a shape. The squares should have **no gaps or overlaps**.

[[☃ image 1]]

is $1$ **square unit** because it measures $1$ unit on each side.

This shape has $9$ square units covering it, not $11$:


[[☃ image 1]]

So, this shape has an area of $11$ square units.

choices.content: ![A figure made up of two rows of equal-sized squares connected together. The top row has five squares and the bottom row has four squares.](web+graphie:${☣ LOCALPATH}/images/3e471d59862c8a54a658f2e4c5e44eaa3dfa37c2)

![A figure made up of three rows of equal-sized squares connected together. The top row has one square, the middle row has three squares, and the bottom row has five squares.](web+graphie:${☣ LOCALPATH}/images/cc7b9a8d33a01010101d5544998cb8ac865489bc)

assessment_items.question_content:
**Which shapes have an area of $15$ square units?**

![A square.](web+graphie:${☣ LOCALPATH}/images/7e744eac00a89cf4ac865b83356eaf7f0c1de260) is $1$ square unit.

[[☃ radio 1]]
hints:
**Area** is the space a flat shape fills.

To measure area, we can count the number of **same-sized** square units needed to cover a shape. The squares should have **no gaps or overlaps**.

![A square.](web+graphie:${☣ LOCALPATH}/images/7e744eac00a89cf4ac865b83356eaf7f0c1de260) is $1$ **square unit** because it measures $1$ unit on each side.

This shape has $12$ square units covering it, not $15$:

![A figure made up of three rows of equal-sized squares connected together. The first row has four squares, the second row has four squares, and the third row has four squares.](web+graphie:${☣ LOCALPATH}/images/c82e9932413427ea10d9ce56a09b32d1a0998753)

This shape has $9$ square units covering it, not $15$:

![A figure made up of three rows of equal-sized squares connected together. The first row has four squares, the second row has four squares, and the third row has four squares.](web+graphie:${☣ LOCALPATH}/images/5d69200f571647373549c110b4c2e9978412182b)

This shape has $15$ square units covering it. There are no gaps or overlaps.

![A figure made up of five rows of equal-sized squares connected together. The first row has three squares, the second row has three squares, the third row has three squares, the fourth row has three squares, and the fifth row has three squares.](web+graphie:${☣ LOCALPATH}/images/05651b6b324bfd040a976d746f507f6f423d0719)

So, this shape has an area of $15$ square units.

This shape has $15$ square units covering it. There are no gaps or overlaps.

![A figure made up of three rows of equal-sized squares connected together. The first row has five squares, the second row has five squares, and the third row has five squares.](web+graphie:${☣ LOCALPATH}/images/a8e2ff2142ed183a183301efc6ae93b9b2b5b19a)

So, this shape has an area of $15$ square units.

These shapes have an area of $15$ square units:  

![There are two figures.  The first is a figure made up of five rows of equal-sized squares connected together. The first row has three squares, the second row has three squares, the third row has three squares, the fourth row has three squares, and the fifth row has three squares. The second is a figure made up of three rows of equal-sized squares connected together. The first row has five squares, the second row has five squares, and the third row has five squares.](web+graphie:${☣ LOCALPATH}/images/05651b6b324bfd040a976d746f507f6f423d0719) ![](web+graphie:${☣ LOCALPATH}/images/a8e2ff2142ed183a183301efc6ae93b9b2b5b19a)

choices.content: ![A figure made up of five rows of equal-sized squares connected together. The first row has three squares, the second row has three squares, the third row has three squares, the fourth row has three squares, and the fifth row has three squares.](web+graphie:${☣ LOCALPATH}/images/05651b6b324bfd040a976d746f507f6f423d0719)))

![A figure made up of three rows of equal-sized squares connected together. The first row has four squares, the second row has four squares, and the third row has four squares.](web+graphie:${☣ LOCALPATH}/images/c82e9932413427ea10d9ce56a09b32d1a0998753)

![A figure made up of five rows of equal-sized squares connected together. The first row has three squares, the second row has one square, the third row has one square, the fourth row has one square, and the fifth row has three squares.](web+graphie:${☣ LOCALPATH}/images/5d69200f571647373549c110b4c2e9978412182b)

![A figure made up of three rows of equal-sized squares connected together. The first row has five squares, the second row has five squares, and the third row has five squares.](web+graphie:${☣ LOCALPATH}/images/a8e2ff2142ed183a183301efc6ae93b9b2b5b19a)
